title: Standardize proof envelope wire format with IANA content type

artifacts.description: Add §4 ABNF grammar for proof envelope, define application/bolyra-proof+json and application/bolyra-proof+cbor media types, document version negotiation semantics

JSON Schema for versioned proof envelope: version, circuitId, provingSystem enum (groth16|plonk), publicSignals[], proof (pi_A/pi_B/pi_C or PLONK equivalents), metadata (chain, registryAddress, timestamp)
ProofEnvelope type definition, serializeEnvelope() and deserializeEnvelope() for both +json and +cbor variants, version negotiation helper, schema validation
Re-export ProofEnvelope types and serialize/deserialize helpers from public API surface
Round-trip serialization tests for +json and +cbor, schema validation rejection tests, version mismatch handling, backward-compatibility with legacy ad-hoc blobs
Canonical test vectors for both media type variants across groth16 and plonk proving systems, one vector per (circuitId, provingSystem, contentType) combination
Add content-type negotiation section showing Accept header usage, envelope construction example, and CBOR vs JSON tradeoffs

steps: Audit existing proof object shapes in sdk/src/ and integrations/ to inventory all ad-hoc serialization patterns that must be unified

Define ProofEnvelope v1 schema fields: version (semver string), circuitId (URI), provingSystem ('groth16'|'plonk'), publicSignals (string[]), proof ({pi_A, pi_B, pi_C} | {A, B, C, Z, T1, T2, T3, Wxi, Wxiw, eval_a, eval_b, eval_c, eval_s1, eval_s2, eval_zw, eval_r, protocol}), metadata ({chain: number, registryAddress: 0x-string, issuedAt: unix-ms})
Write JSON Schema (spec/proof-envelope-schema.json) with strict additionalProperties: false and enum constraints on provingSystem
Draft ABNF grammar in spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md §4 covering envelope structure, field ordering rules, and content-type suffix semantics (+json vs +cbor)
Register placeholder IANA media type names application/bolyra-proof+json and application/bolyra-proof+cbor in the IETF draft per RFC 6838 §4.2 conventions
Implement serializeEnvelope(envelope, format: 'json'|'cbor'): Buffer and deserializeEnvelope(buf, contentType: string): ProofEnvelope in sdk/src/envelope.ts; use cbor-x for CBOR encoding
Add schema validation on deserialization using ajv with the JSON Schema, throwing typed BolyraEnvelopeError with error codes on schema mismatch or unknown version

Update proveHandshake() and verifyHandshake() in sdk/src/index.ts to return and accept ProofEnvelope instead of raw proof objects; maintain a legacy shim for callers passing raw objects (emit deprecation warning)

Generate canonical test vectors covering (HumanUniqueness+groth16+json), (AgentPolicy+groth16+json), (AgentPolicy+plonk+json), (Delegation+groth16+cbor) with known-good publicSignals and proof fields; commit to spec/conformance/proof-envelope-vectors.json
Write round-trip tests in sdk/test/envelope.test.ts: serialize then deserialize equals original, CBOR encoding is smaller than JSON, invalid version string throws, unknown provingSystem rejects
Add conformance runner entry in spec/ that validates all test vectors against the JSON Schema and the ABNF parser
Update sdk/QUICKSTART.md with a content negotiation example and note on when to prefer CBOR (bandwidth-constrained on-chain calldata) vs JSON (REST APIs, debugging)
Run npm run build and npm run typecheck in sdk/; fix any type errors introduced by the ProofEnvelope refactor
Bump sdk package version to 0.3.0 in sdk/package.json to signal the breaking type change, update CHANGELOG.md
dependencies: cbor-x npm package for CBOR serialization
ajv npm package for JSON Schema validation
Existing circuits/build/ .vkey.json files to extract canonical publicSignals lengths per circuit
spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md must exist and have §4 placeholder
RFC 6838 (media type registration) and RFC 7049/8949 (CBOR) as normative references in the IETF draft
